summary: Full e2e testing of Taksh Crafted Veg Experience. Backend: 11/11 pytest tests pass (root, reservations CRUD + validation, contact CRUD + validation, _id excluded). Frontend: home hero, all 8 navbar links, reserve CTA, reservations submit (valid + missing-field error + DB persistence verified), contact submit (valid + missing-field error), menu category filters (all 6 chips), gallery tabs + lightbox open/close, testimonials prev/next carousel — all working. No console errors detected. App is production-ready for the tested flows.

issues: Background goes very dark below the success card on desktop because the left sticky image column is hidden after success — large empty area appears (cosmetic only)
Auto-advance interval (6.5s) can race with manual prev/next clicks during quick interactions — consider pausing the auto-advance for ~10s after a manual click for better UX

critical_code_review_comments: server.py correctly excludes _id via projection ({"_id": 0}) on all list endpoints — good

Reservations frontend swallows specific 422 errors: only reads err.response.data.detail[0].msg, but on a generic network error it falls back to 'Something went wrong. Please call us.' — acceptable for MVP
Reservation date/time fields are plain strings (no server-side date format validation). Pydantic accepts any string. Consider validating YYYY-MM-DD and HH:MM regex if stricter validation is needed
No rate-limiting on /api/reservations and /api/contact — acceptable for MVP but worth noting for production
All key interactive elements have data-testid attributes — testability is excellent
seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_-prefixed reservation and contact rows in MongoDB (test_database) — left in place for inspection; safe to clean up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_taksh_backend.py covers all reservation + contact + root endpoints (11 tests, all green). LoadingScreen overlay shows for ~2.5s — wait 5s after page.goto before interacting. Custom cursor is pointer-events:none so does not block clicks; still prefer data-testid selectors. Menu filter and testimonials carousel use Framer Motion AnimatePresence mode='wait' — wait ~1500ms after click before asserting DOM count/text. Testimonials auto-advances every 6.5s, which can interfere with manual prev/next assertions on long sequences.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Two early intermittent test signals (menu 'After All' returning 4 items, testimonials prev returning unchanged text) were caused by AnimatePresence transition timing and the 6.5s auto-advance interval respectively, NOT product defects. Re-run with 1500ms post-click waits showed correct behaviour.
